company_info: Bank of America is a leading multinational bank and financial services holding company headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina. It offers a full range of banking, investing, asset management, and other financial products and services to individuals, small- and middle-market businesses, large corporations, and governments worldwide.
company_goals: Bank of America is focused on improving financial lives by providing comprehensive banking, investment management, and risk management products and services. It aims to empower clients, employees, and communities to achieve new possibilities and supports economic mobility by creating access to living-wage jobs and fostering community growth. The company serves a diverse client base through physical branches, ATMs, and digital platforms.
key_investors: Berkshire Hathaway

person_info: Ariel Cohen is the Co-Founder and CEO of Navan, a corporate travel and expense management company. He has a strong background in product management and leadership, having held senior roles at Lyft, Jive Software, HP Software, and Mercury Interactive. He co-founded StreamOnce, which was acquired by Jive, and holds an EMBA from Northwestern University's Kellogg School of Management and a BA in Economics from Israel Business College.
company_info: Navan, founded in 2015 by Ariel Cohen and Ilan Twig, is a technology company offering an AI-powered platform that integrates travel booking, corporate-issued payment cards, expense reporting, and analytics for businesses worldwide. Headquartered in Palo Alto, California, Navan simplifies corporate travel and expense management with solutions like Navan Travel, Navan Expense, and Navan Connect.
company_goals: Navan aims to transform business travel and expense management by providing an all-in-one AI-driven platform that streamlines booking, payments, and expense reconciliation. It serves finance, human resources, travel managers, and employees, focusing on delivering a seamless user experience and operational efficiency for enterprises globally.
total_funding: Approximately $2.2 billion raised over multiple rounds since 2015, including a $300 million Series G round in October 2022 at a $9.2 billion valuation, and a $923 million IPO in October 2025.
latest_round: IPO on October 30, 2025, raising $923 million.
key_investors: Andreessen Horowitz, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Greenoaks, Coatue, Zeev Ventures, Addition, Goldman Sachs, Premji Invest

person_info: Azalia Mirhoseini is the Founder and CTO of Ricursive Intelligence. She previously worked as a Senior Staff Research Scientist at Google DeepMind and holds a PhD in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Rice University. She is also an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Stanford University.
company_info: Ricursive Intelligence is a frontier AI lab founded in December 2025 by Azalia Mirhoseini and Anna Goldie. The company focuses on pioneering AI for chip design, assembling a team from top tech companies like Google DeepMind, Anthropic, NVIDIA, Apple, and Cadence.
company_goals: Ricursive Intelligence is building an AI stack that designs, verifies, and closes silicon chips, aiming to automate and accelerate chip design processes. Their vision is to transition from "fabless" to "designless" chip manufacturing, enabling any type of chip to be built automatically using AI. They serve the semiconductor and AI hardware industries, advancing AI capabilities by creating more powerful chips.
latest_round: Series A, $335 million raised by February 2026
key_investors: Sequoia Capital

person_info: Corinne Riley is a Partner at Greylock, a leading venture capital firm. She specializes in early-stage technology investments, focusing on disruptive technologies, enterprise software, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and product-led growth applications.
company_info: Greylock Partners is a leading venture capital firm based in Silicon Valley, founded in 1965. It focuses on being the first institutional partner for AI-first founders, investing primarily in early-stage companies in consumer and enterprise software.
company_goals: Greylock invests in pre-seed, seed, and Series A stage startups, with over 80% of investments being the first check. The firm partners with founders to build companies that matter, offering a distinctive 3-month company-building program called Greylock Edge, and serves entrepreneurs creating their first enterprise software and AI companies.
latest_round: 16th fund ($1 billion) - 2020
key_investors: Reid Hoffman, David Sze, Mustafa Suleyman

person_info: Antoine Blondeau is a technology investor and visionary entrepreneur with a deep background in Artificial Intelligence since 1998. He is the Co-Founder and Managing Partner at Alpha Intelligence Capital (AIC) since 2017 and serves as an advisor to major firms like KKR and board chair of crypto.com. He has international experience, having lived and worked in six countries.
company_info: Alpha Intelligence Capital (AIC) is a global closed-end private venture capital fund led by entrepreneurs and investing in deep Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning technology companies from Seed to Series B stages. Since 2017, AIC has invested in over 30 deep AI companies focused on health and sustainability sectors.
company_goals: Alpha Intelligence Capital aims to invest in and support deep AI/ML technology companies that drive financial returns and positive societal impact, focusing on innovations that contribute to a healthier and more sustainable world. It serves entrepreneurs and startups in the AI space globally, helping them scale and succeed.
latest_round: The round was led by Airtree with strategic participation from Quadrant Private Equity and the National Reconstruction Fund Corporation (NRFC), alongside existing backers including Main Sequence, KKR, IQT, and Alpha Intelligence Capital itself.
key_investors: Airtree, Quadrant Private Equity, National Reconstruction Fund Corporation (NRFC), Main Sequence, KKR, IQT, Alpha Intelligence Capital

person_info: Damien Wattez is the Director of AI and Innovation at SNCF Voyageurs, a role he took on after joining SNCF Voyageurs recently. He brings experience from previous roles at SNCF and OUI.sncf, and holds a background in engineering from 1995 to 2000.
company_info: SNCF (Société Nationale des Chemins de Fer Français) is France's state-owned railway operator, operating nearly all rail transport in France and Monaco, including the TGV high-speed train network.
company_goals: SNCF operates a vast network of high-speed, regional, and local train services across France and into neighboring nations. It is building and deploying the next generation of TGV trains, including the Avelia Horizon, to offer more modern, eco-friendly, and spacious travel with 740 passenger capacity, while serving over 3 billion passengers since 1981.

person_info: Dylan Patel is the Founder, CEO, and Chief Analyst at SemiAnalysis. He is a self-taught semiconductor industry expert who started as a beekeeper and forum enthusiast, growing SemiAnalysis from a solo blog into a leading independent research firm with a global team. His deep technical analysis influences major industry players like Nvidia and AMD.
company_info: SemiAnalysis is an independent investment research firm specializing in the semiconductor industry. It provides deep technical analysis, consulting, and model services, supported by a global team and a chip teardown lab. The firm is recognized for its authoritative insights into semiconductor supply chains and AI infrastructure.
company_goals: SemiAnalysis aims to deliver in-depth, technical research and analysis to semiconductor companies, cloud service providers, and startups. It focuses on tracking semiconductor supply chains, AI infrastructure build-out, and providing strategic insights to influence industry decisions. The company serves hyperscalers, semiconductor giants, startups, and institutional subscribers, working toward expanding its influence and building a venture capital arm.

person_info: Avi Golan is Chief Product & Engineering Officer at Alice, overseeing its GenAI safety and security product offering. With 25+ years of product and engineering leadership, he previously built ZipRecruiter’s Israel R&D hub and served as VP engineering at Checkout.com.
company_info: Alice is a private investment firm that actively chases opportunity via direct co-investments, private equity, and real estate throughout the United States.
company_goals: Alice is focused on private equity and real estate investments, leveraging direct co-investments to pursue opportunities across the U.S. market. The company aims to grow its investment portfolio through strategic partnerships and active management in private equity and real estate sectors.

company_title: Managing Director EMEA & CEO Pro Design
person_info: Duncan Clark is Managing Director EMEA and CEO Pro Design at Canva, overseeing the professional design tools suite including Affinity, Cavalry and Flourish. He is also cofounder of data storytelling platform Flourish, which was acquired by Canva in 2022, and previously worked as a journalist and author focusing on energy and climate change.
company_info: Canva is an online design and visual communication platform that provides a graphic design tool with a mission to empower everyone in the world to design anything and publish anywhere. It offers simplified design tools for creating visual content including social media content, videos, posters, websites, and multimedia presentations.
company_goals: Canva is building and expanding its AI-powered design platform, including tools like Magic Studio, to democratize design and visual communication. The company aims to reach 1 billion users and is working toward competing with Google and Microsoft in the office software category with products like website and whiteboard tools, serving individual users, students, small businesses, and large enterprises including Fortune 500 companies.
latest_round: Series F, $8M, May 01, 2024
key_investors: Blackbird Ventures, Sequoia Capital, General Atlantic, Felicis Ventures, T. Rowe Price, Dragoneer Investment Group

person_info: Barak Kaufman is a Partner at Vine Ventures, where he builds, invests in, and supports mission-driven startups. He previously served as Chief Strategy Officer at Wonderful, an enterprise AI agent platform, and has a background in strategy and operations with experience at TikTok.
company_info: Wonderful is an AI platform provider that delivers enterprise customer interaction automation through AI agents, operating across 30 countries in Europe, Latin America, and Asia-Pacific.
company_goals: The company is building a horizontal enterprise AI foundation to deliver human-grade agents across multiple use cases and workflows, with a focus on non-English-speaking markets. It serves enterprises by deploying tailored AI customer service agents and scaling operations to more countries.
latest_round: Series B - $150M on Mar 12, 2026
key_investors: Insight Partners, Index Ventures, IVP, Bessemer Venture Partners, Vine Ventures

person_info: Dan Lahav is the CEO and Co-Founder of Irregular, a frontier AI security lab. He previously worked as an AI researcher at IBM, where he received the Outstanding Technical Achievement Award, and at LabPixies, a startup acquired by Google. He also served as a lecturer at Tel Aviv University and was the chief adjudicator of the 2021 World Universities Debating Championships in South Korea.
company_info: Irregular is the first frontier security lab dedicated to protecting increasingly capable and sophisticated AI systems. It builds next-generation defenses through high-fidelity research platforms that simulate and monitor real-world AI security scenarios.
company_goals: Irregular runs controlled simulations exposing advanced AI models to realistic threat scenarios, including cyberattacks and autonomous offensive operations, to identify vulnerabilities prior to public deployment. The company aims to become the foundational security layer for the AI era, serving AI labs and government organizations.
latest_round: Series A, $80M, September 17, 2025
key_investors: Sequoia Capital, Redpoint Ventures, Assaf Rappaport (Wiz CEO), Ofir Ehrlich (Eon CEO)

person_info: Dimitris Bountolos is the Chief Information and Innovation Officer at Ferrovial, a Nasdaq-listed infrastructure company. He has a background as an engineer in civil engineering, and has held leadership roles at Iberia Airlines, Latam Airlines, and as a digital advisor to NASA. He is also an entrepreneur who has founded several technology startups.
company_info: Ferrovial is a Spanish multinational infrastructure company operating in transportation and mobility with four divisions: Highways, Airports, Construction, and Mobility and Energy Infrastructure. It operates in over 15 countries with more than 22,600 employees.
company_goals: Ferrovial builds and operates large-scale infrastructure projects including roads, airports, and energy facilities. It works with public and private sector clients to develop sustainable solutions, and is focused on innovation, digital transformation, and reducing environmental impact.

person_info: Dan Wright is the Co-Founder and CEO of Armada, with over 15 years of experience in technology leadership, software development, and product management. He has previously held executive roles at DataRobot and AppDynamics and is known for leading innovative and scalable technology solutions.
company_info: Armada is the world’s first full-stack edge computing platform, providing modular data centers and software solutions that enable edge computing in the most demanding environments globally. Their flagship product, the Galleon, is a ruggedized, mobile data center deployed in remote and harsh locations, integrated with Starlink connectivity.
company_goals: Armada is building a global network of resilient, secure, and scalable edge computing infrastructure to bridge the digital divide and enable AI and compute solutions anywhere on Earth. They serve customers in defense, energy, emergency response, and other sectors requiring reliable edge computing, working toward delivering sovereign AI infrastructure and distributed intelligence.
latest_round: Series B, $100 million, announced in 2023
key_investors: Founders Fund, 137 Ventures, General Catalyst

person_info: Bola Malek is a Head of Baseten for Labs at Baseten, bringing experience from previous roles at Baseten, Rivian, and MIT Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL). She holds a Master of Science in Physics from Massachusetts Institute of Technology and a Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley.
company_info: Baseten is an AI infrastructure company that provides a serverless inference platform for deploying, serving, and scaling machine learning models. It enables developers and enterprises to run open-source, custom, and fine-tuned models in production with tools for high-performance AI inference.
company_goals: Baseten is building a unified AI infrastructure layer to power multi-model strategies combining frontier models with custom and post-trained models. It serves AI-driven enterprises, healthcare providers, and consumer-facing companies by helping them deploy AI products quickly and reliably with up to 40% cost savings on inference.
latest_round: Series F, $1.5 billion, June 2026
key_investors: Altimeter Capital, Conviction, Spark Capital, Sands Capital, Wellington Management, IVP, Greylock, 01A, Blackbird, Durable Capital Partners, Verified Capital, Battery Ventures, DE Shaw Ventures

person_info: Abhishek Das is the Co-founder and Co-CEO of Yutori, an AI startup focused on building autonomous AI agents that can browse and act on the web for users. He was previously a PhD student at Georgia Tech and a Research Scientist at Meta's FAIR team, with expertise in deep reinforcement learning and embodied AI agents.
company_info: Yutori is a San Francisco-based AI technology company developing personal AI assistants capable of autonomously handling everyday digital tasks on the web. The company builds AI agents that can reliably execute tasks such as scheduling, managing finances, and monitoring the web for information users care about.
company_goals: Yutori is building AI agents that serve as personal 'chiefs of staff' to help users reclaim their attention by automating routine web-based tasks. They focus on creating reliable, accurate, and autonomous AI agents that can perform complex actions on the web, aiming to transform how people interact with the internet and improve productivity for individuals.
latest_round: Seed round of $15 million in 2025 led by Radical Ventures with participation from Felicis Ventures and notable angel investors including Fei-Fei Li and Jeff Dean.
key_investors: Radical Ventures, Felicis Ventures, Elad Gil, Sarah Guo, Sandhya Venkatachalam, Jeff Dean, Fei-Fei Li, Amjad Masad, Guillermo Rauch, Akshay Kothari, Logan Kilpatrick, Bryan McCann, Michele Catasta, Shashank Kumar, Vladlen Koltun, Jamie Cuffe, Jon Turow

person_info: Ashwin Sreenivas is the Co-Founder and President at Decagon, a conversational AI company. He previously co-founded Helia, an AI startup focused on real-time video, which was acquired by Scale AI in 2020, and worked as a deployment strategist at Palantir Technologies.
company_info: Decagon is an American artificial intelligence company that develops conversational AI agents for customer service, targeting large enterprises and fast-growing startups across industries including retail, travel, financial services, and health.
company_goals: Decagon is building AI agents that handle customer service interactions across chat, email, voice, and SMS, aiming to replace traditional ticketing systems with personalized, always-on concierge experiences. The company serves global brands like Avis Budget Group, Chime, and Oura Health, helping them scale AI-driven customer service efficiently.
latest_round: Series D - $250M, January 2026
key_investors: Coatue Management, Index Ventures, a16z, A*, Accel, Bain Capital Ventures, Elad Gil, Forerunner, Ribbit Capital, ChemistryVC, Definition Capital, Starwood Capital

person_info: Edwin Chen is the founder and CEO of Surge AI, an AI data annotation company. He studied mathematics, computer science, and linguistics at MIT and previously worked as a machine learning engineer at Facebook, Dropbox, Google, Twitter, and Peter Thiel's hedge fund Clarium.
company_info: Surge AI is an American data annotation company based in San Francisco, California, that focuses on reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), RL environments, and annotating language data.
company_goals: Surge AI provides human evaluation services for AI model outputs, including supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), to help major AI companies like OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Anthropic train more reliable and aligned large language models.
latest_round: In July 2025, Surge AI initiated its first external fundraise, seeking up to $1 billion in a mix of primary and secondary capital at a valuation exceeding $15 billion.
key_investors: Andreessen Horowitz, Warburg Pincus, TPG Inc.

person_info: Alex Atallah is the Co-founder and CEO of OpenRouter. He previously co-founded OpenSea, the world's largest NFT marketplace, where he served as CTO and helped scale the platform to a multi-billion dollar valuation. He has a strong background in software engineering and entrepreneurship, with experience at Palantir Technologies and as a Y Combinator alumnus.
company_info: OpenRouter is a New York City-based AI infrastructure startup founded in 2023. It provides a unified API platform that allows developers and enterprises to access and manage hundreds of AI models from various providers through a single interface. The platform optimizes for cost, speed, and performance, offering enterprise-grade reliability, governance, and controls.
company_goals: OpenRouter is building a multi-model AI routing and optimization platform that serves developers, startups, and large enterprises. It aims to help users avoid vendor lock-in by providing a neutral gateway to hundreds of AI models, optimizing inference costs and performance, and supporting complex AI workflows across software development, data analysis, and customer support.
latest_round: Series B, $113 million, May 2026, led by CapitalG (Alphabet's growth fund)
key_investors: CapitalG, Andreessen Horowitz, Menlo Ventures, NVentures (NVIDIA), ServiceNow Ventures, MongoDB Ventures, Snowflake Ventures, Databricks Ventures

person_info: Dr. Vijay Shilpiekandula is an investor at Dilation Capital, a hedge fund manager focused on industrial and technology sectors. Specific career background details about Dr. Shilpiekandula could not be found in the provided information.
company_info: Dilation Capital Management LP is a US-based hedge fund manager founded in 2019 that provides investment advisory services, portfolio management, and consulting. It invests in shares of industrial and technology companies globally.
company_goals: The company seeks investment opportunities in publicly-traded companies with strong management teams and major secular growth drivers, focusing on sectors like telecommunications, media and technology, industrials, business services, and payments. It serves clients in the United States, primarily pooled investment vehicles.

person_info: Anas Biad is a Partner at Sequoia Capital based in London, focusing on early-stage and growth-stage technology investments across Europe and the U.S. He joined Sequoia in 2021, having previously worked at Silver Lake and Bain, and holds degrees from École Polytechnique, HEC Paris, and Télécom Paris. He is passionate about partnering with founders who have unique insights and are obsessed with winning.
company_info: Sequoia Capital is a venture capital firm that helps daring founders build legendary companies from idea to IPO and beyond. It provides capital and strategic guidance, supporting product development, company building, and market expansion. The firm works closely with leadership teams throughout the business lifecycle and has a portfolio including many unicorns and IPOs.
company_goals: Sequoia Capital is building and working toward supporting innovative technology companies, especially in AI and enterprise software, helping founders scale their businesses globally. It serves early-stage to late-stage startups and growth companies, focusing on long-term partnerships and investing in disruptive technologies that transform how we live and work.
total_funding: Sequoia Capital has raised approximately $231 billion in total funding across its portfolio companies, with a recent $7 billion fund raised in 2026 for AI and late-stage investments.
latest_round: The most recent fund is a $7 billion expansion strategy fund raised in 2026, focused on AI and late-stage investments in the US and Europe.
key_investors: Notable investors include institutional investors such as Finorpa and Re-Sources Capital.

person_info: Cory Hawkvelt is the Chief Product and Technology Officer (CPTO) at NexGen Cloud, where he has been since 2022. Prior to this, he worked as a System Engineer at Calibreone from 2004 to 2011, managing IT infrastructure for multiple companies across five states.
company_info: Hyperstack is an NVIDIA GPU-accelerated cloud platform launched by NexGen Cloud, providing on-demand GPU-as-a-Service for AI, machine learning, and high-performance computing workloads. It is designed for European scale-ups and enterprises, offering self-service access to NVIDIA GPUs with private, sovereign infrastructure.
company_goals: Hyperstack aims to democratize access to GPU cloud computing in Europe by offering cost-effective, sustainable, and compliant infrastructure. It serves AI startups, developers, and enterprises needing scalable compute for AI, HPC, and graphics, with a focus on data sovereignty and reducing carbon emissions through renewable energy.

person_info: Chetan Puttagunta is a General Partner at Benchmark, a venture capital firm. He previously worked at New Enterprise Associates (NEA), where he led investments in MongoDB, MuleSoft, and Elastic, and served on their boards. He joined Benchmark in 2018 as a General Partner.
company_info: Benchmark is an early-stage venture capital firm founded in 1995, known for its equal partnership structure and focus on investing in transformative technology companies. The firm invests in consumer, marketplaces, open-source, AI, infrastructure, and enterprise software sectors.
company_goals: Benchmark works alongside entrepreneurs building startups into transformational companies, focusing on early-stage venture investing in mobile, marketplaces, social, infrastructure, and enterprise software. The firm aims to identify visionary founders and provide them with resources to build enduring companies, with a current focus on AI and enterprise software.
latest_round: Fund X raised $425 million in 2024
key_investors: Bill Gurley (General Partner Emeritus), Peter Fenton (General Partner), Eric Vishria (General Partner), Sarah Tavel (General Partner), Miles Grimshaw (General Partner), Victor Lazarte (General Partner)

person_info: Amritansh Raghav is the Chief Technology Officer at Asana, overseeing technical strategy and engineering execution to support Asana's vision for the Agentic Enterprise. He has over 30 years of experience in engineering, product, and design, with previous leadership roles at Outreach, Skype, Microsoft, and Google. He holds a Master's in Electrical Engineering and a Bachelor's in Electronics and Communication Engineering.
company_info: Asana, Inc. is an American software company based in San Francisco that provides a web and mobile work management platform designed to help teams organize, track, and manage their work. Founded in 2008, Asana offers solutions for project management, team collaboration, and productivity across various industries and serves over 131,000 paying customers globally.
company_goals: Asana is building an operating system for human-agent teams, integrating AI Teammates, AI Studio, and other AI-driven tools to enable organizations to run critical workflows collaboratively between humans and AI agents. The company aims to help teams work smarter, move faster, and deliver results by connecting work to company goals and orchestrating mission-critical workflows. It serves enterprises, small businesses, nonprofits, startups, and government agencies across multiple sectors.
latest_round: Series E round of $51 million on November 29, 2018
key_investors: Generation Investment Management, Andreessen Horowitz, Benchmark, Founders Fund, G Squared

person_info: Grant Lee is the Co-Founder and CEO of Gamma, based in the San Francisco Bay Area. He has a strong background in strategic finance, product understanding, and market insight, and is recognized for his leadership and business acumen.
company_info: Gamma is a company focused on innovative product design and AI-driven solutions, with a significant portion of its early team being product designers. It aims to bring back the feeling of imagination in presentations and rebuilds around AI technologies.
company_goals: Gamma is building AI-powered tools and agents to enhance creativity and productivity, serving creators and professionals who seek to improve their presentation and communication workflows. The company is working toward integrating advanced AI to transform how users create and share content.

person_info: Ariel Shulman is a software engineer currently working as a Full Stack Engineer at Factify since May 2025. He has previous experience as a software developer at illumex and Sisense, and has worked as a freelance web developer and at Bank Hapoalim in Tel Aviv.
company_info: Bright Data is the world's leading web data collection platform, formerly known as Luminati Networks. It provides a comprehensive web data platform including proxy networks, AI-powered web scraping tools, and data intelligence suites, serving over 20,000 customers globally including Fortune 500 companies and academic institutions.
company_goals: Bright Data is building advanced web data infrastructure to enable ethical, legal, and scalable access to public web data. It serves businesses across industries for AI training, market research, e-commerce intelligence, and cybersecurity, aiming to make web data accessible and actionable for smarter decisions.
latest_round: Series C
key_investors: EMK Capital

person_info: Chris Wigley is the Chief Operating Officer at PhysicsX, bringing deep experience in building and scaling technology organizations at the intersection of AI, science, and industry. He was formerly the CEO of Genomics England and COO of QuantumBlack, and most recently founded and served as CEO of Aneira Health.
company_info: PhysicsX is a deep-tech company of scientists and engineers that develops machine learning applications to accelerate physics simulations and enable a new frontier of optimization opportunities in design and engineering. The company was founded in 2019 by former Formula 1 engineers Jacomo Corbo and Robin Tuluie.
company_goals: PhysicsX is building an AI-native engineering platform that replaces conventional physics simulations with AI models delivering results in seconds. The platform is used across aerospace, automotive, semiconductors, materials, and energy sectors to help engineering teams explore vastly larger design spaces and optimize complex systems.
latest_round: Series C, $300M, June 8, 2026
key_investors: Temasek, NVIDIA, Siemens, Applied Materials, Atomico, General Catalyst

person_info: Franck Greverie is the Chief Technology & Portfolio Officer and Group Executive Board Member at Capgemini, responsible for leading the portfolio of offers and product management, technology management and innovation, and overseeing six global business lines including data & AI, Business Services, and Cloud & Cybersecurity. He joined Capgemini in 2015 as Head of the Cybersecurity Global Service Line and previously held senior leadership roles at Bull, Thales Group, and Alcatel.
company_info: Capgemini is a French multinational information technology (IT) services and consulting company headquartered in Paris, France, established in 1967. It provides consulting, technology, and digital transformation services to help organizations achieve business transformation.
company_goals: Capgemini is building and working toward becoming a global leader in AI, data, and digital transformation, focusing on agentic AI, quantum computing, and intelligent industry. It serves clients across industries including aerospace, automotive, banking, healthcare, and public sector, helping them reimagine and transform their business operations.

person_info: George Kurian is the Chief Executive Officer and a member of the Board of Directors of NetApp. He joined the company in 2011 and succeeded Tom Georgens as CEO on June 1, 2015.
company_info: NetApp, Inc. is an American data infrastructure company that provides unified data storage, integrated data services, and cloud operations (CloudOps) solutions to enterprise customers.
company_goals: NetApp is building and working toward an intelligent data infrastructure that unifies data storage and integrated data services across hybrid and multi-cloud environments. The company serves 84% of Fortune 500 companies, as well as enterprises in finance, healthcare, and government, by helping them build a data fabric that delivers the right data, to the right place, at the right time, with the right capabilities.
latest_round: Series D, $800M on Sep 25, 2017
key_investors: Sequoia Capital, Greenleaf Trust

person_info: Ed Huang is a backend engineer specializing in cloud-native environments and distributed systems. He has experience designing scalable real-time transaction systems and optimizing performance with microservices and event-driven architectures.
company_info: PingCAP is an open-source distributed Hybrid Transactional/Analytical Processing (HTAP) database software developer, creating TiDB, a distributed SQL database that serves as a one-stop solution for online transactions and analysis.
company_goals: PingCAP is building and scaling TiDB, an advanced open-source, distributed SQL database with MySQL compatibility, to serve modern applications requiring elastic scaling, real-time analytics, and hybrid transactional and analytical processing. It serves growing businesses across technology, financial services, travel, Web3, and gaming, as well as global innovation leaders like Databricks, Block, and Pinterest.
latest_round: Series D, $270M, May 2021
key_investors: 5Y Capital, GGV Capital, Fosun, Coatue, GIC

person_info: Dillon Rolnick is the CEO of Nous Research, an open-source AI research lab focused on developing human-centric language models and simulators. He has been active in the AI community, promoting decentralized AI and open-source initiatives, and has collaborated with major tech companies like NVIDIA and Microsoft.
company_info: Nous Research is a decentralized AI startup that focuses on developing open-source, human-centric artificial intelligence models and tools. The company works on large language model architecture, data synthesis, fine-tuning, and reasoning, aiming to align AI systems with real-world user experiences.
company_goals: Nous Research is building open-source language models like the Hermes series and infrastructure like the Psyche Network for decentralized AI training. The company serves the open-source AI community, including researchers, developers, and practitioners seeking to leverage or build upon advanced language models, with a long-term vision of advancing natural language processing through rigorous research and open collaboration.
latest_round: Series A - $50M in April 2025
key_investors: Paradigm, Distributed Global, OSS Capital, Delphi Ventures, North Island Ventures

company_title: Senior Director of Data Acquisition, QA, and Platform
person_info: Fred de Villamil is the Senior Director of Technology at NIQ for Digital Commerce, where he leads the technology and engineering teams. He has a background in data and analytics, having previously served as Chief Technology Officer at Data Impact by NielsenIQ.
company_info: NIQ (NielsenIQ) is a global marketing research firm and the world's largest consumer intelligence provider, offering end-to-end solutions from digital measurement to activation across all channels. It serves brands, retailers, and e-commerce companies with data-driven insights.
company_goals: NIQ is building AI-driven commerce solutions, including NIQ Product Intelligence and NIQ Connected Content, to transform fragmented product data into structured, interoperable intelligence. These solutions help brands and retailers fuel AI discovery, recommendations, and agentic commerce across digital and physical channels.
key_investors: Advent International

person_info: Charles Zedlewski is the Chief Product Officer at Together AI, a role he was welcomed to in February 2025. He previously served as Chief Operating Officer at Temporal Technologies and has a strong background in product leadership, having helped Cloudera grow from a pre-product startup to a multi-product public company with nearly $800 million in ARR.
company_info: Together AI is a cloud-based platform designed for constructing open-source generative AI and infrastructure for developing AI models. The company contributes cutting-edge open-source research, models, and datasets to push the boundaries of AI forward.
company_goals: Together AI is building the AI Native Cloud, a full-stack AI platform that powers inference, fine-tuning, and GPU clusters. They serve developers, AI-native startups, and global enterprises by providing serverless and dedicated inference, model fine-tuning, GPU clusters, and agentic workflow capabilities across more than 200 open-source models.
latest_round: Series B - $305M on Feb 20, 2025
key_investors: General Catalyst, Prosperity7 Ventures, Salesforce Ventures, NVIDIA, Kleiner Perkins, Coatue, Lux Capital, Emergence Capital, Definition, Cadenza Ventures, Long Journey Ventures, Brave Capital, Scott Banister, John Chambers

person_info: David Bitton is the Vice-President AI and Product Strategy at 5C Group, a role he has held since 2024. He is a technology entrepreneur with over 28 years of experience, previously co-founding three companies including 3vis, which became Canada's leading Autodesk M&E partner.
company_info: 5C Group is one of North America's largest AI digital infrastructure providers, offering purpose-built AI infrastructure solutions for frontier labs, AI-native companies, and large-scale enterprise clients. The company combines Hypertec Group's 40 years of expertise in HPC and data center operations with its own AI infrastructure capabilities.
company_goals: 5C Group is building and delivering large-scale, AI-native digital infrastructure solutions, including data centers and AI platforms, to accelerate AI ambitions for clients worldwide. The company serves AI frontier labs, AI-native companies, and large-scale enterprise clients with secure, reliable, and sustainable data center and AI infrastructure solutions.

It offers sovereign AI full stack infrastructure including data center planning, construction, operation, and cloud platforms for GPU-as-a-Service and AI workloads. Polarise is an official NVIDIA Preferred Partner and Cloud Service Provider and partners with Deutsche Telekom for the German Industrial AI Cloud.
company_goals: Polarise is building sovereign, sustainable AI infrastructure to serve the heavy AI workloads of tomorrow with a focus on resource-saving and environmental responsibility. It aims to provide peak performance AI data centers and cloud services tailored to the AI industry, enabling European businesses and public sector entities to deploy AI applications securely and efficiently. The company serves industrial companies, public sector, and enterprises requiring sovereign AI compute power and compliance with European data residency and privacy standards.
latest_round: No specific latest round date found, but total funding is $148 million as of 2026, with significant investment from SWI Group and others.
key_investors: SWI Group, NVIDIA, Deutsche Telekom, Alliance DAO (for related Polarise Protocol)

company_info: Handelsblatt is Germany's leading business and financial newspaper, providing in-depth coverage of business, finance, and economic policy. It is part of the Handelsblatt Media Group, which also offers various media products including newspapers, magazines, and digital content.

company_info: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company focused on building reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. It develops advanced AI models like Claude, designed for reasoning, long-context processing, and enterprise use, emphasizing AI safety and interpretability.

company_info: Ornn is building the financial infrastructure and price discovery layer for the AI compute economy.
